title: Odyssey, Book 10: Circe
introduction: Book 10 of the Odyssey: Circe. The Greek text follows the standard Homeric vulgate.

translation.text: They reached the island of Aeolus, keeper of the winds, who gave Odysseus a bag containing all the winds except the west wind. Within sight of Ithaca, his men opened the bag, and the winds blew them back. They came to the Laestrygonians, who destroyed all ships but one. Then to Circe's island, where the witch turned his men into swine. With Hermes' help, Odysseus resisted her magic and made her restore his men. They stayed a year.
